schema.title: Response Health Check Api V1 Health Get

get.description:
List assets with optional filtering.

The verification / ownership / description filters back the
"governance saved-searches" surface in the UI command palette. When
multiple filters are supplied they AND together.

parameters.name: verified

schema.description: Filter by verification state. true → only verified; false → only unverified.
schema.title: Verified

parameters.description: Filter by verification state. true → only verified; false → only unverified.

parameters.name: has_owner

schema.description: true → assets with at least one incoming `owns` relation; false → orphan assets.
schema.title: Has Owner

parameters.description: true → assets with at least one incoming `owns` relation; false → orphan assets.

parameters.name: has_description

schema.description: true → assets with a non-empty description; false → undocumented assets.
schema.title: Has Description

parameters.description: true → assets with a non-empty description; false → undocumented assets.

get.description: Get a single asset by UID.

post.description: Create a new actor (person or group).

get.description: Get a single actor by UID.

post.description: Create a new relation between two nodes.

get.description: Get a single relation by UID.
Get a single event by UID.
List all rules applied to an asset, with enforcement context per rule.
Get a single rule by UID.

delete.description: Delete a rule (and all APPLIES_TO relations through DETACH DELETE).

get.description:
Search across assets, actors, rules and schema nodes.

Returns a flat `items` list where each entry is tagged with `kind`
(asset / container / field / actor / rule). Empty queries return
an empty list — the UI treats empty search as "show nothing yet".
Return the data-landscape map at the requested LOD tier.

parameters.name: lod

schema.description: Level-of-detail ceiling. 0 = overview (systems + teams only); 1 = full entity map (+ datasets, reports, processes, people, rules).
schema.title: Lod

parameters.description: Level-of-detail ceiling. 0 = overview (systems + teams only); 1 = full entity map (+ datasets, reports, processes, people, rules).

get.description: Return all registered plugin manifests for UI auto-discovery.

post.description:
Run a plugin. Multipart inputs are parsed against the manifest.

- SummaryResult → returned as JSON
- DownloadResult → streamed back with an attachment Content-Disposition

schema.title: Response Run Plugin Api V1 Plugins  Slug  Run Post

get.description: List every tag currently in use, sorted by usage count (descending).

Resolve `uid` to its typed payload, regardless of node label.

The lookup is a single Cypher hop to determine the label, followed
by a typed read through the appropriate service so all the usual
response shaping (JSON metadata decode, datetime conversion, etc.)
runs unchanged. 404 if no node has that uid.

schema.title: Response Get Entity Api V1 Entities  Uid  Get

post.description:
Register a new webhook. The HMAC ``secret`` is returned **once** — the
client must store it now to verify future ``X-Holocron-Signature`` headers.

get.description: Get a single webhook by uid.

put.description: Update a webhook. Setting ``disabled=false`` clears the failure counter.

post.description:
Fire a synthetic event at the webhook so the receiver can be verified.

The event has ``entity_uid="webhook-test"`` and ``metadata.test=true`` so
receivers can filter test traffic out of analytics if they want.

schema.title: Response Test Webhook Api V1 Webhooks  Uid  Test Post

uid.title: Uid
uid.description: Optional client-supplied UID for idempotent creation. Auto-generated if not provided.

ActorCreate.title: ActorCreate
ActorCreate.description: Request body for creating an actor.

ActorHit.title: ActorHit
ActorHit.description: A person or team that matched.

ActorResponse.title: ActorResponse
ActorResponse.description: Response model for a single actor.

ActorUpdate.title: ActorUpdate
ActorUpdate.description: Request body for updating an actor.

AppliedRule.title: AppliedRule
AppliedRule.description: Rule + its per-asset APPLIES_TO context (enforcement, field_path, note).

AppliedRulesResponse.title: AppliedRulesResponse
AppliedRulesResponse.description: List of rules applied to one asset, with enforcement context per rule.

uid.title: Uid
uid.description: Optional client-supplied UID for idempotent creation. Auto-generated if not provided.

AssetCreate.title: AssetCreate
AssetCreate.description: Request body for creating an asset.

AssetHit.title: AssetHit
AssetHit.description: An asset that matched the query.

AssetResponse.title: AssetResponse
AssetResponse.description: Response model for a single asset.

AssetUpdate.title: AssetUpdate
AssetUpdate.description: Request body for updating an asset.

ContainerHit.title: ContainerHit
ContainerHit.description: A schema container (table, sheet, section, …) that matched.

EntityActorResponse.title: EntityActorResponse
EntityActorResponse.description: Polymorphic envelope for an Actor hit.

EntityAssetResponse.title: EntityAssetResponse
EntityAssetResponse.description: Polymorphic envelope for an Asset hit.

EntityRuleResponse.title: EntityRuleResponse
EntityRuleResponse.description: Polymorphic envelope for a Rule hit.

EntityType.title: EntityType
EntityType.description: Types of entities that can be tracked.

EventResponse.title: EventResponse
EventResponse.description: Response model for a single event.

FieldHit.title: FieldHit
FieldHit.description: A schema field (column, measure, …) that matched.

GraphEdge.title: GraphEdge
GraphEdge.description:
A relation between two map nodes. Both endpoints are always visible
at their declared `lod`; edges inherit the higher of the two tiers.

bounds.title: Bounds
bounds.description: Layout bounding box in 3D: (x_min, y_min, z_min, x_max, y_max, z_max).

GraphMapResponse.title: GraphMapResponse
GraphMapResponse.description: The data-landscape map at a given LOD ceiling.

subtype.title: Subtype
subtype.description: Asset type, actor type, or rule severity — drives node color

lod.description: Lowest LOD tier at which this node becomes visible

degree.title: Degree
degree.description: Relation count — drives hub glow on the 3D renderer

GraphNode.title: GraphNode
GraphNode.description:
A node on the map, with its pre-computed world coordinates.

Coordinates are in a 3D space: `(x, y)` is the galactic plane,
`z` is the layer offset so LOD tiers visually stack (tier 0 sits on
`z≈0`, tier 1 spreads in a thin shell around it). A 2D renderer can
ignore `z` entirely.

InputSpec.title: InputSpec
InputSpec.description:
One input the plugin expects in `run()`.

Drives both the API (multipart parsing) and the UI (form rendering).

LodTier.title: LodTier
LodTier.description:
Level-of-detail tiers.

`tier 0` is the "max zoom out architecture view" — only top-level
entities (systems + teams). `tier 1` adds every first-class node
(assets, people, rules). Future `tier 2` would add schema nodes
(containers + fields).

PluginCapability.title: PluginCapability
PluginCapability.description:
What kind of action the plugin performs.

- IMPORT: takes inputs (often a file), pushes assets/actors/relations to the API,
  returns a JSON summary that the UI renders as stat cards + a sample list.
- EXPORT: produces a file download (no inputs needed in v0.1).

slug.title: Slug
slug.description: URL-safe identifier — must be unique across plugins

PluginManifest.title: PluginManifest
PluginManifest.description: Self-description of a plugin. Returned to the UI for auto-rendering.

uid.title: Uid
uid.description: Optional client-supplied UID for idempotent creation. Auto-generated if not provided.

RelationCreate.title: RelationCreate
RelationCreate.description: Request body for creating a relation.

RelationResponse.title: RelationResponse
RelationResponse.description: Response model for a single relation.

RelationType.title: RelationType
RelationType.description:
Valid relation types.

Data-flow lineage is asset-only: the single FEEDS edge covers
upstream → downstream flow between any two assets (including
processes, which participate as plain asset nodes). We intentionally
do not carry PRODUCES/CONSUMES or DERIVED_FROM so the same data flow
never has two parallel encodings.

uid.title: Uid
uid.description: Optional client-supplied UID for idempotent creation. Auto-generated if not provided.

RuleCreate.title: RuleCreate
RuleCreate.description: Request body for creating a rule.

RuleResponse.title: RuleResponse
RuleResponse.description: Response model for a single rule.

RuleSeverity.title: RuleSeverity
RuleSeverity.description: How bad a rule violation is, inherent to the kind of rule.

RuleUpdate.title: RuleUpdate
RuleUpdate.description: Request body for updating a rule. All fields optional.

TagListResponse.title: TagListResponse
TagListResponse.description:
Distinct tags currently in use across the catalog. Sorted by
count descending, then name ascending — so a typeahead UI can show
the most-common tags first while staying deterministic.

name.title: Name
name.description: Lowercase tag name as stored on the asset.

count.title: Count
count.description: Number of assets carrying this tag.

TagUsage.title: TagUsage
TagUsage.description:
A tag that's currently in use on at least one asset, with its
catalog-wide usage count. Counts let UI consumers prefer the
dominant spelling when offering suggestions (`pii (12)` vs.
`PII (1)`).

url.title: Url
url.description: HTTPS URL the API will POST events to.

events.title: Events
events.description: Event topics to subscribe to. Use ``['*']`` for every event, or a list like ``['asset.created', 'actor.updated']``.

secret.title: Secret
secret.description: HMAC key used to sign each request (header ``X-Holocron-Signature``). Auto-generated when omitted; the generated secret is returned once on creation.

WebhookCreate.title: WebhookCreate
WebhookCreate.description: Request body for registering a new webhook subscription.

secret.title: Secret
secret.description: HMAC key — store now, the API will not surface it again.

WebhookCreateResponse.title: WebhookCreateResponse
WebhookCreateResponse.description: Webhook + plaintext secret. Only returned once, at creation time.

WebhookResponse.title: WebhookResponse
WebhookResponse.description: Webhook returned by the API. ``secret`` is only ever exposed on creation.

WebhookUpdate.title: WebhookUpdate
WebhookUpdate.description: Partial update for an existing webhook. All fields optional.
